scope: Fix attempt — detail:high for sub-resolution text
note: detail:high is now the parser default (clear general win: at 'low' the model couldn't even attempt the micro-text). But it STILL misreads 4pt -> '6pt' — the vision API downsamples a full page to a fixed budget no matter the detail, so only region-tiling (zoomed crops) would fix it. Deemed not worth building: the real corpus has no sub-resolution text; use Azure DI for exactness in prod.
Garbled text layer that broke Docling came out clean — in one call, no OCR, no local models.

scope: French accents (é è à ç ô, Hölder)
note: Accents perfect, including Hölder which Docling's OCR mangled to 'Holder'.
Rebuilt the table exactly; rowspan cell left blank; wrapped cell and PENDING* kept.
Described both as figures and did NOT invent chart data — the big hallucination trap, avoided.
Ignored the grid (no structural decay); captured overlapping text; didn't invent the clipped off-page part.
Misread 'exactly 4pt' -> '6pt' and 'This text' -> 'The text'. Higher DPI did NOT help — the vision API downsamples to a token budget. Needs detail:high / region-tiling.
Misread 'INTERNAL R&D USE ONLY' -> 'INTERNAL FOR USE ONLY'. Low-contrast text guessed plausibly wrong.

scope: Color (red PENDING*, blue text) & bordered box
note: Color is dropped (Markdown can't carry it; prompt didn't ask). Bordered box rendered as a code block — a formatting artifact.
